title: Communauté de communes du Pays de l'Ourcq
summary: The Communauté de communes du Pays de l'Ourcq is a federation of municipalities (communauté de communes) in the Seine-et-Marne département and in the Île-de-France région of France. Established on 13 December 1973, its seat is Ocquerre. Its area is 234.7 km2, and its population was 17,546 in 2018.
text:
The Communauté de communes du Pays de l'Ourcq is a federation of municipalities (communauté de communes) in the Seine-et-Marne département and in the Île-de-France région of France. Established on 13 December 1973, its seat is Ocquerre. Its area is 234.7 km2, and its population was 17,546 in 2018.

Composition
The communauté de communes consists of the following 22 communes:
